steps.title: Use a two-point exponential fit
steps.content: "150% more" means the growth factor is $1+1.50=2.50$. Since the end-of-2019 count is $175$ and $t=1$ then, the starting value is $$\frac{175}{2.5}=70.$$

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $n=a(b)^t$. The starting value is $a=70$, and the growth factor is $b=2.5$, so the equation is $n=70(2.5)^t$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Verify by checking $t=1$: $70\times2.5=175$. The matching choice has the correct starting value and growth factor, so B is correct.
choices: • $n=70(1.5)^t$ — uses a growth factor of $1.5$, which would represent a 50% increase, not 150% more. Eliminated.
• The matching choice: $n=70(2.5)^t$ — growth factor $2.5$ correctly reflects 150% more, and $n_0 = 70$ is confirmed by back-solving with $n = 175$ at $t = 1$. B is correct.
• $n=175(1.5)^t$ — wrong growth factor and uses the year-2019 count as the starting value, shifting the timeline. Eliminated.
• $n=175(2.5)^t$ — correct factor but wrong initial value; this would give $n_0 = 175$ at $t = 0$ (end of 2018), contradicting the problem. Eliminated.
